issues.objection: Tags on 7.21 (operator-system-coupling, deliberate-action, iteration-loop) cohere with same-tag verses across chapters. Coheres with Ch 3's positive case for shipping (the engineer who deeply commits is acting, not retreating); coheres with Ch 4.34's discipleship voice (the disciplined commitment over time produces the substrate-knowing). STRETCHED tagging keeps the operational scope honest where metaphysical reach exceeds it. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Verse claims a personal divine agency (vidadhāmy aham — I make firm); engineering analog stays at structural-pattern scope. Counter-example explicitly carves out structurally-inappropriate technique-fit so the analog does not collapse into 'commit to anything.'

notes: Doctrine-coherence test for 7.21: same-tag verses across chapters preserve consistent stance; the verse extends the doctrine without contradicting prior chapters. PASS.
